title: Multi-Objective Semantics (normative meta-contract)
description: Normative behavioral contract for how implementations must normalize and aggregate multiple objectives. This document is META-CONTRACT documentation, NOT a wire-format schema: the fields below are not transmitted in API payloads. Implementations (Python SDK, traigent-js SDK, TraigentBackend, TraigentFrontend) must hard-code the const values listed here. The schema exists so behavior is discoverable and testable across the four codebases that consume objective definitions; it does not validate any request or response body. Wire-format objective fields are defined in objective_definition_schema.json.

zero_span_fallback.description: Value returned by min-max normalization when |max - min| < zero_span_epsilon for an objective. 0.5 is a neutral midpoint: an all-equal trial set carries no information for that objective, so every trial contributes equally to the weighted sum. NOT a runtime field; implementations hard-code this constant.

zero_span_epsilon.description: Single normative tolerance for detecting zero-span. All implementations must use exactly this value, not '<= this'. Spans below this collapse to zero_span_fallback; spans above this are normalized linearly. NOT a runtime field; implementations hard-code this constant.

weight_normalization.description: Relative objective weights are rescaled so Sigma w_i = 1 before aggregation. Weights flow on the wire in their raw user-supplied form (see objective_definition_schema.json#/properties/weight); rescaling happens at ObjectiveSchema construction time inside each SDK. NOT a runtime field.

dominance_guard.description: Design-time validation that prevents a single objective from dominating a multi-objective schema. NOT a runtime field; implementations enforce this at ObjectiveSchema construction.

max_normalized_weight.description: Maximum allowed normalized weight (after sum-to-one rescaling) for any single objective when objectives.length > 1. Exceeding this raises a validation error at schema construction. Matches Python SDK's MAX_SINGLE_OBJECTIVE_WEIGHT.

validation_scope.description: METADATA about which codebases enforce dominance_guard at schema construction. NOT a wire-format field. Today only the Python and JS SDKs enforce the guard; TraigentBackend's interactive_session_service objective normalizer validates positive weights only, and TraigentFrontend trusts already-validated payloads. Future expansion to ['sdk', 'backend'] is filed as a follow-up; this field documents current scope so consumers do not assume universal enforcement.
